_patch_meta.description: Dimension review patch — C17 Reg 068 (grace): all 11 OWNER-term groups assigned CLAUDE_AI confidence and dominant_subject; 2 dimension corrections (889-003 Cognition→Moral Character; 890-001 Relational Disposition→Dependence/Creatureliness); registry-level stamp set. Cluster stamp NOT set — remaining C17 registries deferred to normal cluster processing.

set.notes: God's gracious giving freely — charizō as the act of the divine disposition of favour expressed in total gift (the Son, forgiveness, all things). Relational Disposition confirmed: the settled divine orientation of goodwill that gives without condition. Dominant subject: GOD throughout 7-verse corpus.

Human act of forgiving — releasing another from debt, patterned on divine forgiveness received. The inner-relational act of grace extended person-to-person. Relational Disposition confirmed: inner orientation of releasing rather than holding. Dominant subject: HUMAN (the forgiver in Eph 4:32 and throughout 8-verse corpus).

Divine bestowing of grace — charitoō as the act by which God marks and transforms the recipient through favour (Luk 1:28 perfect passive: kecharitōmenē). Relational Disposition confirmed: divine relational act creating a condition of standing. Dominant subject: GOD as the one who bestows.

Grace as sovereign, unmerited divine disposition toward humanity — the freely given gift through which God justifies, saves, and sustains (Eph 2:8: not your own doing; 2Cor 12:9: sufficient in weakness). Relational Disposition confirmed: the settled divine orientation of favour that precedes and is not conditioned by merit. Dominant subject: GOD throughout 22-verse corpus.

Grace as the relational sphere and ongoing condition in which the believer stands and lives (Rom 5:2: access into this grace in which we stand; 1Cor 15:10: by grace I am what I am). Relational Disposition confirmed: the sustained inner-relational condition of the human person shaped by grace. Dominant subject: HUMAN — the believer's ongoing inner condition is the primary characteristic engaged.

Grace as relational favour — the find-favour idiom (heuriskō charin) naming the disposition of goodwill that opens access, creates loyalty, reflects the giver's character (Luk 1:30: found favour with God). Relational Disposition confirmed: inner orientation of goodwill constituting the favour relationship. Dominant subject: GOD (anchor explicitly divine; group spans divine-human and human-human contexts but anchor frames divine favour).

Grace as animating power and sphere of apostolic mission — the word of grace, ministry identity, commendation to grace (Acts 20:32). The inner-being characteristic is the human orientation toward and participation in the grace-sphere. Relational Disposition confirmed over Agency/Power: the power in view is grace operating through the relational bond, not the person's own capacity. Dominant subject: HUMAN — the apostolic person's inner orientation sourced in grace.

Divine relational favour based on prior personal knowledge — chen as God's disposition toward those he knows by name (Exo 33:17: found favour... I know you by name). Also Zec 12:10: Spirit of grace poured out producing mourning and supplication — pneumatological dimension noted. Relational Disposition confirmed. Dominant subject: GOD — both anchors show divine action.

Interpersonal relational favour — chen as goodwill one person discovers in another, unexpectedly and without merit (Rut 2:10: foreigner finding favour). Relational Disposition confirmed. Dominant subject: OTHER_HUMAN — the bearer of the characteristic is the one granting favour (Boaz toward Ruth); the inner disposition of goodwill belongs to the other person.

Favour as inner character quality — chen as gracious disposition expressed in speech, conduct, and standing in community (Pro 31:30: charm deceitful, beauty vain; the fear of the Lord is what endures). DIMENSION CORRECTED from Cognition (KEYWORD_WEAK keyword match on 'wisdom'). Moral Character is correct: chen here names an inner character quality belonging to a person, not a cognitive faculty. Dominant subject: HUMAN.

records.description: 889-003 — grace (chen): CORRECTED Cognition → Moral Character | HUMAN

set.notes: Earnest supplication — ta.cha.nun as the inner act of plea arising from deep need and relational vulnerability before God (Dan 9:3: prayer and pleas with fasting, sackcloth, ashes; Zec 12:10: Spirit of grace producing pleas for mercy). DIMENSION CORRECTED from Relational Disposition: the primary inner-being characteristic is not the relational bond itself but the creature's orientation of recognised need and dependence before the greater. Dependence/Creatureliness names this more precisely. Dominant subject: HUMAN — the supplicant throughout.

records.description: 890-001 — grace (ta.cha.nun): CORRECTED Relational Disposition → Dependence/Creatureliness | HUMAN

record.finding: Group 890-001 (ta.cha.nun, supplication) — Dan 9:3 anchor shows earnest supplication enacted somatically: fasting, sackcloth, ashes. The body enacts what the inner person is doing — the whole person brought into alignment with the inner posture of plea. Session B should examine the somatic signature of grace-supplication: whether the body-posture vocabulary of petition (prostration, fasting, weeping, raised hands) encodes inner-being content about the Dependence/Creatureliness orientation, and how this connects to the grace registry's inner-being picture. Zec 12:10 (shared anchor with 889-001) shows that the capacity for earnest supplication is itself grace-given — the Spirit of grace produces the plea. This is the inner-being grammar of grace-response: grace first, supplication as grace-enabled response.

records.description: DIM-068-001: somatic signature of supplication and grace-supplication causal chain

record.description: Zec 12:10 is an anchor verse in both 889-001 (chen — divine relational favour) and 890-001 (ta.cha.nun — supplication). The same verse connects: (1) God pouring out a spirit of grace (chen — Relational Disposition, GOD), and (2) the human response of pleas for mercy (ta.cha.nun — Dependence/Creatureliness, HUMAN). This shared anchor encodes the inner-being grammar of grace-response: divine grace-disposition produces the human supplication-orientation. The causal chain — grace outpoured → mourning → supplication — is a structural insight spanning two inner-being characteristics (relational favour and creaturely dependence) within the same registry. Session D should examine whether this chain appears across other C17 registries and whether it constitutes a programmatic inner-being pattern in the divine-human relational cluster.

records.description: DIM-068-SD001: Zec 12:10 shared anchor — grace-disposition producing supplication-response chain

Registry 068 (grace): Dimension Review complete under v1.9 — registry-level stamp set. Note: cluster stamp for C17 NOT set in this patch; remaining C17 registries deferred to normal cluster processing session.
